iterations.theme: Sistemska korupcija i kontinuitet elita kao temeljni problem Hrvatske

sections.subtitle: Kamo ide Hrvatska? Vangelić o korijenima problema
sections.screenshot_description: Voditelj i Damir Vangelić na početku podcasta Mrežnica.
sections.content: Voditelj podcasta Mrežnica, na samom početku razgovora, postavlja ključno pitanje: "Kamo i kojim smjerom ide Hrvatska?" Kao gosta, po prvi puta u podcastu, predstavlja Damira Vangelića, istaknutog poduzetnika i menadžera s bogatom poslovnom karijerom, poznatog po tome što "nema dlake na jeziku". Voditelj ga opisuje i kao "nesuđenog hrvatskog premijera", sugerirajući da bi s više sreće možda i preuzeo tu ulogu. Vangelić prihvaća poziv na otvoren razgovor, spreman detaljno analizirati goruće probleme hrvatskog društva.
sections.subtitle_en: Where is Croatia going? Vangelić on the roots of the problem
sections.screenshot_description_en: Host and Damir Vangelić at the beginning of the Mrežnica podcast.
sections.content_en: The host of the Mrežnica podcast, at the very beginning of the conversation, asks a key question: "Where and in what direction is Croatia going?" As a guest, for the first time in the podcast, he introduces Damir Vangelić, a prominent entrepreneur and manager with a rich business career, known for "not having hair on his tongue". The host also describes him as "an unappointed Croatian prime minister", suggesting that with more luck he might have taken on that role. Vangelić accepts the invitation for an open conversation, ready to thoroughly analyze the burning problems of Croatian society.

sections.subtitle: Dugotrajna sjena prošlosti: Kako komunističke elite i dalje oblikuju Hrvatsku
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vangelić objašnjava kontinuitet elita u Hrvatskoj.
sections.content: Damir Vangelić odmah u početku razgovora ističe da je temeljni problem Hrvatske **kontinuitet komunističkih elita** koji traje već osamdeset godina. Pozivajući se na znanstvene radove, objašnjava da je 1989. godine Komunistička partija Hrvatske imala oko 320.000 članova. Značajan dio tih članova, oko 17%, prešao je u SDP, dok je dvostruko veći broj, uključujući najjače kadrove poput socijalističkih direktora i pripadnika tajnih službi, prešao u HDZ. Vangelić naglašava da je pomirba, koju je inicirao Franjo Tuđman, trebala biti privremena, ali se nastavila, što je dovelo do situacije u kojoj se očekuje da će isti ljudi koji su stvorili probleme, te probleme i riješiti – što je, prema njegovom mišljenju, nerealno. Ove elite se, iako se u ciklusima obnavljaju novim licima, drže iste paradigme, što onemogućuje stvarni napredak.
sections.subtitle_en: Long-lasting shadow of the past: How communist elites still shape Croatia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vangelić explains the continuity of elites in Croatia.
sections.content_en: Damir Vangelić immediately at the beginning of the conversation points out that the fundamental problem of Croatia is the **continuity of communist elites** which has lasted for eighty years. Referring to scientific works, he explains that in 1989, the Communist Party of Croatia had about 320,000 members. A significant portion of these members, about 17%, transferred to the SDP, while a twice larger number, including the strongest cadres such as socialist directors and members of secret services, transferred to the HDZ. Vangelić emphasizes that the reconciliation, which Franjo Tuđman initiated, was supposed to be temporary, but it continued, which led to a situation in which it is expected that the same people who created the problems will also solve those problems – which is, in his opinion, unrealistic. These elites, although they are renewed in cycles with new faces, adhere to the same paradigm, which prevents real progress.

sections.subtitle: Hrvatska u raljama disfunkcionalnosti: Od sporog pravosuđa do iznenadnog bogatstva
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić gestikulira dok nabraja znakove disfunkcionalnosti države.
sections.content:
Vangelić detaljno opisuje brojne znakove disfunkcionalne države, ističući da su se građani na njih već navikli, što je poznati psihološki fenomen. Kao najjači signal izdvaja **korupciju**, koja se manifestira ne samo financijski, već i kroz nepotizam i zapošljavanje po vezi. Pravosuđe je nefunkcionalno i sporo, a kao primjer navodi revizije u poljoprivredi gdje su postupci trajali u prosjeku 828 dana, što je, kako kaže, "životni vijek" za mnoge tvrtke. Ostali znakovi uključuju:
- Duge liste čekanja
- Porast javnog duga
- Državne investicije bez smisla
- Povećanje državnog aparata
- Pojavu "novih oligarha" – ljudi koji su prije deset godina imali manje od deset tisuća eura, a danas posjeduju milijarde.
Svi ovi elementi zajedno čine sliku države koja ne funkcionira u interesu svojih građana.
sections.subtitle_en: Croatia in the jaws of dysfunctionality: From slow judiciary to sudden wealth
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić gesticulates while enumerating signs of the state's dysfunctionality.
sections.content_en:
Vangelić describes in detail numerous signs of a dysfunctional state, emphasizing that citizens have already become accustomed to them, which is a known psychological phenomenon. As the strongest signal, he singles out **corruption**, which manifests not only financially, but also through nepotism and employment through connections. The judiciary is dysfunctional and slow, and as an example, he cites audits in agriculture where proceedings lasted an average of 828 days, which is, as he says, "a lifespan" for many companies. Other signs include:
- Long waiting lists
- Increase in public debt
- State investments without sense
- Increase in the state apparatus
- The emergence of "new oligarchs" – people who ten years ago had less than ten thousand euros, and today own billions.
All these elements together form a picture of a state that does not function in the interest of its citizens.

sections.subtitle: Egzodus iz Hrvatske: Korupcija tjera više od rata
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić objašnjava razloge iseljavanja iz Hrvatske, s naglaskom na korupciju.
sections.content:
Na pitanje voditelja je li korupcija najveća rak-rana hrvatskog društva, Vangelić potvrđuje, pozivajući se na brojna znanstvena istraživanja među hrvatskim iseljenicima. U posljednjih dvanaest godina, iz Hrvatske je otišlo preko 520.000 ljudi, što je, prema Vangeliću, veći egzodus nego za vrijeme turskih osvajanja. Ironično primjećuje da je "vlada Andreja Plenkovića puno jača nego bilo koji Osmanlija ikad", jer je otjerala više Hrvata. Ključno je da **korupcija** nije samo financijski problem, već je u svim istraživanjima navedena kao **najjači razlog iseljavanja**, dok je financijski razlog tek na trećem ili četvrtom mjestu. Ljudi odlaze jer:
- Se ne mogu zaposliti bez veze ("već je dogovoreno da Pero je primljen").
- Moraju biti članovi stranke čak i za posao čistačice u vrtiću.
- Vide da sudstvo ne funkcionira i da se krade.
- Gube povjerenje u budućnost Hrvatske.
Za razliku od Slovaka ili Poljaka, kojima je financijska razlika bila ogromna pri odlasku u inozemstvo, Hrvatima je ta razlika bila manja, što dodatno potvrđuje da su nematerijalni faktori, poput korupcije i nepravde, ključni za njihov odlazak.
sections.subtitle_en: Exodus from Croatia: Corruption drives more than war
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić explains the reasons for emigration from Croatia, with an emphasis on corruption.
sections.content_en:
To the host's question whether corruption is the biggest cancer of Croatian society, Vangelić confirms, referring to numerous scientific studies among Croatian emigrants. In the last twelve years, over 520,000 people have left Croatia, which is, according to Vangelić, a greater exodus than during the Turkish conquests. He ironically notes that "the government of Andrej Plenković is much stronger than any Ottoman ever," because it drove away more Croats. It is crucial that **corruption** is not just a financial problem, but is listed in all studies as the **strongest reason for emigration**, while the financial reason is only in third or fourth place. People leave because:
- They cannot get a job without connections ("it has already been agreed that Pero is hired").
- They have to be party members even for a cleaning job in a kindergarten.
- They see that the judiciary does not function and that theft occurs.
- They lose trust in the future of Croatia.
Unlike Slovaks or Poles, for whom the financial difference was enormous when going abroad, for Croats that difference was smaller, which further confirms that non-material factors, such as corruption and injustice, are crucial for their departure.

sections.subtitle: Vrh piramide korupcije: Kako lideri štite podobne, a uklanjaju poštene
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić govori o odgovornosti vrha vlasti i primjeru Nataše Tramišak.
sections.content: Damir Vangelić jasno ističe da je za stanje u državi uvijek kriv **vršni čovjek**, odnosno premijer, jer on diktira tempo i smjer. Kritizira praksu kontinuiranog biranja korumpiranih suradnika, koje se potom hvali i grli, čak i kada su "jednom nogom u Remetincu". S druge strane, napada se i uklanja one koji pokušavaju riješiti probleme. Kao primjer navodi bivšu ministricu **Natašu Tramišak**, koju opisuje kao jednu od najhrabrijih u Plenkovićevoj Vladi. Tramišak je pokušala raskrinkati aferu "Omega software", zbog čega joj se prijetilo, a premijer je te prijetnje omalovažavao. Na kraju je Tramišak uklonjena iz Vlade. Vangelić zaključuje da se sustav rješava svakoga tko želi riješiti stvari, dok zadržava duboko korumpirane, što sugerira da takvi odgovaraju vrhu vlasti jer su poslušni i podložni ucjenama. Ova praksa, koja traje predugo, rezultira okruživanjem premijera "mediokritetima", često polupismenim ljudima bez dokazanog poslovnog iskustva, pa čak i s lažnim diplomama. Brojne smjene i uhićenja suradnika premijera, te svađe s europskom tužiteljicom, jasni su pokazatelji duboke korumpiranosti vrha vlasti.
sections.subtitle_en: Top of the pyramid of corruption: How leaders protect the suitable, and remove the honest
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić speaks about the responsibility of the top of the government and the example of Nataša Tramišak.
sections.content_en: Damir Vangelić clearly points out that for the state of the country, the **top man** is always to blame, i.e., the prime minister, because he dictates the tempo and direction. He criticizes the practice of continuously electing corrupt associates, who are then praised and embraced, even when they are "one foot in Remetinec". On the other hand, those who try to solve problems are attacked and removed. As an example, he cites former minister **Nataša Tramišak**, whom he describes as one of the bravest in Plenković's Government. Tramišak tried to expose the "Omega software" affair, for which she was threatened, and the prime minister belittled those threats. In the end, Tramišak was removed from the Government. Vangelić concludes that the system gets rid of anyone who wants to solve things, while retaining the deeply corrupt, which suggests that such individuals suit the top of the government because they are obedient and susceptible to blackmail. This practice, which has lasted too long, results in the prime minister being surrounded by "mediocrities", often semi-literate people without proven business experience, and even with fake diplomas. Numerous dismissals and arrests of the prime minister's associates, and quarrels with the European prosecutor, are clear indicators of the deep corruption at the top of the government.

sections.subtitle: Sistemska korupcija u praksi: Od rotirki do neplaćenih kampova
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić objašnjava primjere sistemske korupcije, uključujući Maju Đerek i kampove.
sections.content:
Vangelić ilustrira sistemsku korupciju kroz nekoliko konkretnih primjera. Prvi je **fenomen "rotirki"** u Zagrebu, gdje se sve više vozila s plavim svjetlima bahato probija kroz promet, a njihovi putnici se ponašaju superiorno. To je, prema Vangeliću, pokazatelj korumpiranog ponašanja jer se smatraju važnijima od građana koji ih plaćaju. Spominje i primjer ministra koji je ilegalno koristio plava svjetla, na što premijer nije reagirao. S druge strane, premijer reagira na zviždače, poput **Maje Đerek**, koja je bila izložena financijskim, medijskim, pravnim i psihičkim pritiscima jer je tražila naplatu neplaćenih najamnina. Ministar joj je čak sugerirao da "pusti" pet milijuna kuna duga.
Drugi veliki primjer je **neplaćanje najamnina u kampovima**. Stranka Republika, čiji je Vangelić član, podnijela je kaznenu prijavu jer se godinama ne naplaćuju najamnine za kampove, koji su najprofitabilnija djelatnost u Hrvatskoj (osim prodaje droge). Kampovi, smješteni u zaštićenom obalnom pojasu, vrijede milijune, ali država ih ne naplaćuje četiri godine, što je rezultiralo gubitkom desetaka, pa i stotina milijuna eura. Vangelić ističe da u Vladi sjede ljudi čija rodbina posjeduje kampove, a Ministarstvo financija i Ministarstvo prostornog uređenja i graditeljstva ignoriraju ovaj problem, unatoč upozorenjima revizora. To nije nesposobnost, već **sistemska korupcija** gdje se nesposobnost koristi kao alat za koruptivne radnje.
sections.subtitle_en: Systemic corruption in practice: From rotating lights to unpaid camps
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić explains examples of systemic corruption, including Maja Đerek and camps.
sections.content_en: Vangelić illustrates systemic corruption through several concrete examples. The first is the **phenomenon of "rotirke"** in Zagreb, where more and more vehicles with blue lights arrogantly push through traffic, and their passengers behave superiorly. This is, according to Vangelić, an indicator of corrupt behavior because they consider themselves more important than the citizens who pay them. He also mentions the example of a minister who illegally used blue lights, to which the prime minister did not react. On the other hand, the prime minister reacts to whistleblowers, such as **Maja Đerek**, who was exposed to financial, media, legal, and psychological pressures because she sought the collection of unpaid rents. The minister even suggested to her to "let go" of five million kuna of debt. Another major example is the **non-payment of rents in camps**. The Republika Party, of which Vangelić is a member, filed a criminal complaint because rents for camps, which are the most profitable activity in Croatia (besides drug sales), have not been collected for years. Camps, located in the protected coastal belt, are worth millions, but the state has not collected them for four years, which has resulted in a loss of tens, even hundreds of millions of euros. Vangelić points out that people whose relatives own camps sit in the Government, and the Ministry of Finance and the Ministry of Physical Planning, Construction and State Property ignore this problem, despite auditors' warnings. This is not incompetence, but **systemic corruption** where incompetence is used as a tool for corrupt actions.
keywords_en: Ministry of Spatial Planning and Construction
entities_en: Ministry of Spatial Planning and Construction

sections.subtitle: Šokantni razmjeri korupcije i uloga europske tužiteljice Tamare Laptaš
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić govori o razmjerima korupcije i hvali rad Tamare Laptaš.
sections.content:
Vangelić naglašava da su razmjeri korupcije u Hrvatskoj šokantni. Prema istraživanju Europskog parlamenta, koje je surađivalo s nekoliko think tankova, Hrvatska gubi **oko 13,5% BDP-a** zbog krađe javnih sredstava, što iznosi između pet i šest milijardi eura godišnje. Dodatno, analiza procjena šteta za proračun EU od strane Europskog javnog tužitelja pokazuje da Hrvatska prednjači: na milijun stanovnika, iz EU fondova se ukrade oko **145 milijuna eura**, što je dvostruko više od Bugarske, sljedeće najgore zemlje.
U borbi protiv ove sistemske korupcije, Vangelić izdvaja **Tamaru Laptaš**, europsku tužiteljicu u Hrvatskoj, kao "ženu heroja". Unatoč pritiscima, nedostatku zaštite i konstantnim napadima cijelog sustava, uključujući i samog premijera, Laptaš uporno i sustavno radi na uhićenjima. Vangelić ističe da se hrvatske vlasti često klone europskih fondova, a kada dođe do uhićenja, pokušavaju prebaciti nadležnost na domaće pravosuđe, tvrdeći da se radi o sredstvima iz državnog proračuna, čime se implicira da je krađa iz nacionalnog budžeta manje problematična.
sections.subtitle_en: Shocking extent of corruption and the role of European prosecutor Tamara Laptaš
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić speaks about the extent of corruption and praises the work of Tamara Laptaš.
sections.content_en: Vangelić emphasizes that the extent of corruption in Croatia is shocking. According to a study by the European Parliament, which collaborated with several think tanks, Croatia loses **about 13.5% of GDP** due to the theft of public funds, which amounts to between five and six billion euros annually. Additionally, an analysis of damage estimates to the EU budget by the European Public Prosecutor shows that Croatia leads: per million inhabitants, about **145 million euros** are stolen from EU funds, which is double that of Bulgaria, the next worst country. In the fight against this systemic corruption, Vangelić singles out **Tamara Laptaš**, the European prosecutor in Croatia, as a "hero woman". Despite pressures, lack of protection, and constant attacks from the entire system, including the Prime Minister himself, Laptaš persistently and systematically works on arrests. Vangelić points out that Croatian authorities often avoid European funds, and when arrests occur, they try to transfer jurisdiction to the domestic judiciary, claiming that these are funds from the state budget, thereby implying that theft from the national budget is less problematic.

sections.subtitle: Korupcija u praksi: Oprosti dugove, imenuj podobne i izvozi poslove u Kazahstan
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić objašnjava kako korupcija operira kroz vladine odluke i stranačka imenovanja.
sections.content:
Vangelić objašnjava kako korupcija operira na višim razinama, daleko od sitnih mita. Jedan od ključnih mehanizama su **odluke Vlade o oprostu dugova poduzetnicima**, koje prolaze "ispod radara". Takve odluke mogu oprostiti milijune kuna duga, a formalno su donesene na sjednicama Vlade pod izlikom "olakšavanja poslovanja poduzetnicima".
Drugi mehanizam su **stranačka imenovanja** u upravama i nadzornim odborima brojnih državnih tvrtki (preko 1400). Umjesto da se biraju neovisni stručnjaci, kako bi se očekivalo ulaskom u OECD, imenuju se stranački poslušnici. Vangelić predlaže da se u nadzorne odbore imenuju mladi, neopterećeni studenti, bez stranačkih veza, kako bi se prekinuo krug poslušnosti.
Ističe da se oni koji nisu "dobri s premijerom" suočavaju s problemima, dok su onima bliskima sva vrata otvorena. Kao primjer navodi osnivanje tvrtke kćeri Agencije za ugljikovodike, koja će istraživati naftu i plin u **Kazahstanu**. Vangelić to smatra sumnjivim, jer je Kazahstan poznat po korupciji i ne izručuje, a Hrvatska ima vlastite neistražene rezerve nafte i plina (npr. u Slavoniji). Smatra da se takvi poslovi dogovaraju "na janjetini", daleko od očiju javnosti, što je u Kazahstanu puno lakše nego u Hrvatskoj.
sections.subtitle_en: Corruption in practice: Forgive debts, appoint suitable ones, and export business to Kazakhstan
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić explains how corruption operates through government decisions and party appointments.
sections.content_en: Vangelić explains how corruption operates at higher levels, far from petty bribes. One of the key mechanisms are **Government decisions on debt forgiveness for entrepreneurs**, which pass "under the radar". Such decisions can forgive millions of kunas of debt, and are formally made at Government sessions under the pretext of "facilitating business for entrepreneurs". Another mechanism are **party appointments** in the management boards and supervisory boards of numerous state-owned companies (over 1400). Instead of independent experts being chosen, as would be expected with entry into the OECD, party loyalists are appointed. Vangelić suggests that young, unburdened students, without party ties, be appointed to supervisory boards, in order to break the cycle of obedience. He points out that those who are not "on good terms with the prime minister" face problems, while for those close to him, all doors are open. As an example, he cites the establishment of a subsidiary of the Hydrocarbon Agency, which will explore for oil and gas in **Kazakhstan**. Vangelić considers this suspicious, because Kazakhstan is known for corruption and does not extradite, and Croatia has its own unexplored oil and gas reserves (e.g., in Slavonia). He believes that such deals are arranged "over lamb", far from the public eye, which is much easier in Kazakhstan than in Croatia.

sections.subtitle: Milijunska plinska afera: Kako je Škugorova krađa ostala nekažnjena
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić objašnjava detalje afere Škugor i plinske krađe.
sections.content:
Damir Vangelić detaljno opisuje **aferu Škugor** kao paradigmatski primjer sistemske korupcije. Škugor, kojeg Vangelić naziva "tipičnim hadezeovcem" i "potkapacitiranim" menadžerom, unatoč sličnoj aferi s plinom 2006./2007., ponovno je uguran na direktorsku poziciju u INA-i. Kao član Energetskog odbora HDZ-a i zamjenik predsjednika nadzornog odbora Plinare istočne Slavonije, Škugor je omogućio svom kolegi i prijatelju premijera Plenkovića, koji je vodio Odvjetničku komoru, da obavlja pravne usluge za Plinaru.
Istovremeno, Škugor i njegovi suradnici razvili su shemu gdje je plin jeftino prodavan paralelnoj tvrtki, transportiran kroz Plinaru istočne Slavonije, a zatim prodavan po višestruko većoj cijeni. Plinara istočne Slavonije, čiji su vlasnici INA i županija (obje pod kontrolom HDZ-a), zabilježila je skok prihoda s 70 milijuna kuna na milijardu kuna, a nitko u upravi ni nadzornom odboru to navodno nije primijetio. Vangelić se pita zašto INA i Plinara istočne Slavonije nisu podnijele tužbe za naknadu štete protiv Škugora i ostalih menadžera, već čekaju ishod kaznenog postupka, što je, prema njemu, nepovezano. To ukazuje na **zaštitu cijele ekipe**, dok 50 milijuna eura iz afere i dalje nedostaje, a akteri slobodno šeću.
sections.subtitle_en: Million-dollar gas affair: How Škugor's theft remained unpunished
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić explains the details of the Škugor affair and gas theft.
sections.content_en: Damir Vangelić describes in detail **the Škugor affair** as a paradigmatic example of systemic corruption. Škugor, whom Vangelić calls a "typical HDZ member" and an "under-capacitated" manager, despite a similar gas affair in 2006/2007, was again pushed into a directorial position in INA. As a member of the HDZ Energy Committee and deputy chairman of the supervisory board of Plinara istočne Slavonije, Škugor enabled his colleague and friend of Prime Minister Plenković, who led the Bar Association, to perform legal services for Plinara. At the same time, Škugor and his associates developed a scheme where gas was sold cheaply to a parallel company, transported through Plinara istočne Slavonije, and then sold at a multiple times higher price. Plinara istočne Slavonije, whose owners are INA and the county (both under HDZ control), recorded a jump in revenue from 70 million kuna to one billion kuna, and allegedly no one in the management or supervisory board noticed this. Vangelić wonders why INA and Plinara istočne Slavonije did not file lawsuits for damages against Škugor and other managers, but are waiting for the outcome of the criminal proceedings, which, according to him, is unrelated. This indicates **the protection of the entire team**, while 50 million euros from the affair are still missing, and the actors walk freely.

sections.subtitle: Medijska omerta i 'kapilarna korupcija': Kako HDZ kontrolira narativ
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić objašnjava kako se korupcija zataškava kontrolom medija i državnih tvrtki.
sections.content:
Vangelić objašnjava da se afere u Hrvatskoj brzo zataškavaju jer je HDZ "davno shvatio da mora imati pod kontrolom sve medije". To se postiže kroz državne tvrtke koje financiraju medije. Predlaže zabranu dviju stvari za državne tvrtke:
1.  **Sponzoriranje sportskih reprezentacija i saveza:** To je novac građana, a očigledno se koristi za pranje novca.
2.  **Medijsko oglašavanje:** Kao primjer navodi HEP, koji drži 98% tržišta, pa je oglašavanje u medijima poput Jutarnjeg lista ili 24 sata besmisleno s tržišnog aspekta. Svrha je kontrola medija.
Osim toga, tisuće državnih agencija i trgovačkih društava (oko 1400-1500) kapilarno dotiraju manje iznose (npr. 50.000 eura), što se zbraja u milijarde i omogućuje **"kapilarnu korupciju"**.
Vangelić kritizira i nedostatak provjera nesrazmjera imovine i primanja pojedinaca od strane Ministarstva financija, što je ključno za borbu protiv korupcije. Spominje i ukidanje sigurnosnih provjera za ministre i dužnosnike od strane Milanovića, što je Plenković nastavio, čime se otvaraju vrata za ljude s upitnim pozadinama.
sections.subtitle_en: Media omerta and 'capillary corruption': How HDZ controls the narrative
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić explains how corruption is covered up by controlling media and state-owned companies.
sections.content_en:
Vangelić explains that scandals in Croatia are quickly covered up because HDZ "long ago realized that it must have all media under control". This is achieved through state-owned companies that finance the media. He proposes a ban on two things for state-owned companies:
1.  **Sponsoring sports national teams and federations:** This is citizens' money, and it is obviously used for money laundering.
2.  **Media advertising:** As an example, he cites HEP, which holds 98% of the market, so advertising in media like Jutarnji list or 24 sata is meaningless from a market perspective. The purpose is media control.
In addition, thousands of state agencies and commercial companies (around 1400-1500) capillarily subsidize smaller amounts (e.g., 50,000 euros), which adds up to billions and enables **"capillary corruption"**.
Vangelić also criticizes the lack of checks on the disproportion between assets and income of individuals by the Ministry of Finance, which is crucial for fighting corruption. He also mentions the abolition of security checks for ministers and officials by Milanović, which Plenković continued, thereby opening the door for people with questionable backgrounds.

sections.subtitle: Politički teatar: Milanović i Plenković kao 'dva lica istog novčića' i štetni uzori
sections.screenshot_description: Vangelić objašnjava zašto Milanović i Plenković djeluju kao 'dva lica iste stranke'.
sections.content:
Damir Vangelić smatra da su Milanović i Plenković "dva lica istog novčića", odnosno "ista partija" u pozadini, s vrlo sličnim obrascima i ciljevima. Njihovi međusobni prijepori su, prema njemu, "jako dobra predstava". Vangelić tvrdi da je Milanović na prošlim parlamentarnim izborima 2020. godine, svojim postupcima (iako se nije kandidirao za premijera, ali je okupljao koaliciju), zapravo pomogao Plenkoviću da ostane na vlasti, eliminirajući druge opcije.
Kao primjer Milanovićevog "trolanja" i skretanja pažnje, Vangelić navodi situaciju tijekom afere Škugor i plinske krađe. Kada su novinari pitali Milanovića o uhićenjima HDZ-ovaca, on je preusmjerio temu na izjavu "NATO nije trebao bombardirati Beograd", što je postala glavna vijest i zataškalo korupcijski skandal.
Ovakvo ponašanje, uz međusobno vrijeđanje ("životinjska farma"), stvara štetne standarde i loš je primjer za djecu. Vangelić ističe da će Hrvatskoj trebati deset godina da se vrati u normalu, poput Slovenije, i da teži Danskoj. Kritizira i Plenkovićevu vladu zbog zapošljavanja 20.000 novih ljudi u javnoj upravi u deset godina (ekvivalent cijele zgrade "Zagrepčanke"), što dovodi do neefikasnosti i rasta javnog duga, jer se mirovine i plaće pokrivaju zaduženjima.
sections.subtitle_en: Political theater: Milanović and Plenković as 'two sides of the same coin' and harmful role models
sections.screenshot_description_en: Vangelić explains why Milanović and Plenković act as 'two faces of the same party'.
sections.content_en: Damir Vangelić believes that Milanović and Plenković are "two sides of the same coin", i.e., "the same party" in the background, with very similar patterns and goals. Their mutual disputes are, according to him, "a very good show". Vangelić claims that Milanović, in the last parliamentary elections in 2020, through his actions (although he did not run for prime minister, but was gathering a coalition), actually helped Plenković stay in power, eliminating other options. As an example of Milanović's "trolling" and diversion of attention, Vangelić cites the situation during the Škugor affair and gas theft. When journalists asked Milanović about the arrests of HDZ members, he redirected the topic to the statement "NATO should not have bombed Belgrade", which became the main news and covered up the corruption scandal. Such behavior, along with mutual insults ("animal farm"), creates harmful standards and is a bad example for children. Vangelić points out that it will take Croatia ten years to return to normal, like Slovenia, and to strive for Denmark. He also criticizes Plenković's government for employing 20,000 new people in public administration over ten years (the equivalent of the entire "Zagrepčanka" building), which leads to inefficiency and public debt growth, because pensions and salaries are covered by borrowing.

iterations.theme_en: Systemic corruption and the continuity of elites as the fundamental problem of Croatia

iterations.theme: Ekonomske poteškoće, neučinkovitost države i erozija dijaloga

sections.subtitle: Mentalitet 'uhljebljivanja' i strah u društvu: Kondicioniranje nacije
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vanđelić objašnjava kako se Hrvati prilagođavaju sustavu 'uhljebljivanja' iz straha.
sections.content:
Voditeljica je postavila pitanje o mentalitetu 'uhljebljivanja' u Hrvatskoj, sugerirajući da možda i narod sam želi takav sustav. Damir Vanđelić je objasnio da se ne radi o inherentnoj osobini Hrvata, već o **kondicioniranju**. Ističe da se Hrvati, kada odu u inozemstvo, poput Njemačke, lako prilagođavaju redu i postaju dobri radnici. Smatra da je prilagodba kontekstu ključna za preživljavanje, pa se tako ljudi u Hrvatskoj prilagođavaju sustavu gdje se ne smiju zamjeriti premijeru ili moraju prihvatiti da je 'laž istina'.

Vanđelić je naglasio da mnogi članovi HDZ-a, iako nezadovoljni, ne dižu glas zbog straha od političke i profesionalne odmazde. Kao osobni primjer, naveo je odbijanje ponude za kandidaturu za gradonačelnika Zagreba, jer nije želio biti 'maneken' u korumpiranom sustavu. Njegovo odbijanje dovelo je do 'zamjeranja' premijeru Andreju Plenkoviću. Također je spomenuo situaciju bivšeg zagrebačkog gradonačelnika Milan Bandića, sugerirajući da je bio ucijenjen od strane HDZ-a, birajući između zatvora i političke pozicije. Vanđelić zaključuje da ljudi žive u strahu i da su kondicionirani da takvo ponašanje smatraju normalnim, iako to nije.
sections.subtitle_en: Mentality of 'cronyism' and fear in society: Conditioning of the nation
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vanđelić explains how Croats adapt to the system of 'cronyism' out of fear.
sections.content_en: The host asked a question about the 'cronyism' mentality in Croatia, suggesting that perhaps the people themselves want such a system. Damir Vanđelić explained that it is not an inherent characteristic of Croats, but about **conditioning**. He points out that Croats, when they go abroad, like to Germany, easily adapt to order and become good workers. He believes that adaptation to context is crucial for survival, so people in Croatia adapt to a system where they must not displease the prime minister or must accept that 'a lie is the truth'. Vanđelić emphasized that many HDZ members, although dissatisfied, do not speak out due to fear of political and professional retaliation. As a personal example, he cited his refusal of an offer to run for mayor of Zagreb, because he did not want to be a 'model' in a corrupt system. His refusal led to 'displeasure' from Prime Minister Andrej Plenković. He also mentioned the situation of former Zagreb mayor Milan Bandić, suggesting that he was blackmailed by the HDZ, choosing between prison and a political position. Vanđelić concludes that people live in fear and are conditioned to consider such behavior normal, even though it is not.

sections.subtitle: Neodrživ rast BDP-a i nedostatak industrije: Iluzija prosperiteta
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vanđelić objašnjava da rast BDP-a u Hrvatskoj nije održiv zbog nedostatka industrije.
sections.content:
Unatoč hvaljenom rastu BDP-a, Damir Vanđelić upozorava da je taj rast **neodrživ** i temeljen na neprirodnim izvorima. Objašnjava da je visok rast BDP-a posljedica niske početne točke i tri ključna faktora:

- **Europski fondovi:** Značajan priljev sredstava iz EU-a.
- **Državno zaduživanje:** Vlada se zadužuje za realizaciju projekata.
- **Doznake iz inozemstva:** Novac koji šalju hrvatski iseljenici svojoj rodbini.

Vanđelić ističe da Hrvatska nema industrijsku proizvodnju poput Mađarske, Slovačke ili Češke, koje privlače velike tvornice poput Suzukija, BMW-a ili Audija. Glavni razlozi za nedostatak industrije u Hrvatskoj su **neizvjesno i sporo pravosuđe** te **spore javne institucije** (poput HEP-a ili HERA-e), koje investitore odbijaju dugotrajnim procesima i nepoštivanjem rokova. Korupcija, prema Vanđeliću, također igra ključnu ulogu, jer mnogi investitori izbjegavaju zemlje s niskim rejtingom na Transparency International ljestvici. Zaključuje da ovakav rast BDP-a ne može trajati beskonačno, jer će se iseljenici s vremenom odvojiti od Hrvatske, a europski fondovi nisu trajno rješenje zbog neodgovornog zaduživanja Europe.
sections.subtitle_en: Unsustainable GDP growth and lack of industry: Illusion of prosperity
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vanđelić explains that GDP growth in Croatia is not sustainable due to the lack of industry.
sections.content_en:
Despite the praised GDP growth, Damir Vanđelić warns that this growth is **unsustainable** and based on unnatural sources. He explains that the high GDP growth is a consequence of a low starting point and three key factors:

- **European funds:** Significant inflow of funds from the EU.
- **State borrowing:** The government borrows for project realization.
- **Remittances from abroad:** Money sent by Croatian emigrants to their relatives.

Vanđelić points out that Croatia does not have industrial production like Hungary, Slovakia, or the Czech Republic, which attract large factories like Suzuki, BMW, or Audi. The main reasons for the lack of industry in Croatia are **uncertain and slow judiciary** and **slow public institutions** (like HEP or HERA), which deter investors with lengthy processes and non-adherence to deadlines. Corruption, according to Vanđelić, also plays a key role, because many investors avoid countries with a low rating on the Transparency International scale. He concludes that this kind of GDP growth cannot last indefinitely, because emigrants will eventually detach from Croatia, and European funds are not a permanent solution due to Europe's irresponsible borrowing.

sections.subtitle: Unutarnji uzroci propasti industrije: Od skladištara do nekretnina
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vanđelić kritizira imenovanje skladištara na čelo brodogradilišta Uljanik kao primjer lošeg upravljanja.
sections.content:
Damir Vanđelić odbacuje tezu da je propast hrvatske industrije posljedica vanjskih diktata, tvrdeći da bi to značilo da su hrvatski vođe 'jadni i kukavice'. Umjesto toga, naglašava **unutarnje uzroke**. Kao primjer navodi imenovanje skladištara na čelo brodogradilišta Uljanik, što je rezultiralo gubicima. Smatra da je europska politika protiv državnih subvencija za neprofitabilne tvrtke zapravo korisna, jer sprječava trošenje javnog novca na neodržive projekte. Problem leži u tome što se u Hrvatskoj plaća održavanje tvrtki koje žive 'na državnoj sisi'.

Ključni problem vidi u **privatizacijskom modelu** koji je industriju pretvorio u nekretnine. Umjesto razvoja proizvodnje, industrijski pogoni su se rušili kako bi se gradile stambene zgrade i trgovački centri. Vanđelić ističe da u Hrvatskoj postoji mnogo uspješnih tvrtki, poput DOKING-a, koje su profitabilne i inovativne, te da Europa ne bi tražila njihovo zatvaranje. Problem je, dakle, u domaćim odlukama i lošem upravljanju, a ne u vanjskim naredbama.
sections.subtitle_en: Internal causes of the collapse of industry: From warehouse workers to real estate
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vanđelić criticizes the appointment of a warehouse manager to the head of the Uljanik shipyard as an example of poor management.
sections.content_en: Damir Vanđelić rejects the thesis that the collapse of Croatian industry is a consequence of external dictates, claiming that this would mean that Croatian leaders are 'wretched and cowards'. Instead, he emphasizes **internal causes**. As an example, he cites the appointment of a warehouse manager to head the Uljanik shipyard, which resulted in losses. He believes that European policy against state subsidies for unprofitable companies is actually beneficial, as it prevents the spending of public money on unsustainable projects. The problem lies in the fact that in Croatia, the maintenance of companies that live 'on the state's teat' is paid for. He sees the key problem in the **privatization model** that transformed industry into real estate. Instead of developing production, industrial plants were demolished to build residential buildings and shopping centers. Vanđelić points out that there are many successful companies in Croatia, such as DOKING, which are profitable and innovative, and that Europe would not demand their closure. The problem is, therefore, in domestic decisions and poor management, and not in external orders.

sections.subtitle: Inflacija i pogrešne vladine mjere: Tko je odgovoran?
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vanđelić objašnjava da je Vlada RH odgovorna za inflaciju u Hrvatskoj.
sections.content:
Damir Vanđelić jasno poručuje da inflacija u Hrvatskoj ima 'ime i prezime' – a to je **Vlada Republike Hrvatske**. Objašnjava da je inflacija rezultat povećanja količine novca u optjecaju na istu količinu roba, za što su odgovorni oni koji sjede u Vladi. Kritizira Vladu zbog ubacivanja silnih količina novca u sustav i zapošljavanja tisuća novih zaposlenika, što usporava procese i smanjuje efikasnost. Kao primjer navodi da je ishođenje građevinskih dozvola danas dvostruko dulje nego prije petnaest godina, unatoč digitalizaciji i većem broju zaposlenih.

Posebno se osvrnuo na vladine 'socijalne' mjere, poput kontrole cijena goriva i određenih prehrambenih proizvoda. Smatra da su te mjere neefikasne i kontraproduktivne, jer subvencioniraju turiste i kamionski promet, umjesto da ciljano pomažu najugroženijima. Predlaže **austrijski model vaučera** za gorivo i plin, koji bi omogućio ciljanu pomoć obiteljima s niskim primanjima, umjesto neselektivnog snižavanja cijena koje iskrivljuje tržište. Vanđelić naglašava da Vlada, regulirajući cijene pojedinih proizvoda, ne uspijeva kontrolirati tržište, već stvara nestašice ili promjene u pakiranju proizvoda, što je, kako kaže, 'suludo' i 'Staljin nije uspio'.
sections.subtitle_en: Inflation and wrong government measures: Who is responsible?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vanđelić explains that the Government of the Republic of Croatia is responsible for inflation in Croatia.
sections.content_en: Damir Vanđelić clearly states that inflation in Croatia has 'a first and last name' – and that is the **Government of the Republic of Croatia**. He explains that inflation is the result of an increase in the amount of money in circulation for the same amount of goods, for which those who sit in the Government are responsible. He criticizes the Government for injecting huge amounts of money into the system and employing thousands of new employees, which slows down processes and reduces efficiency. As an example, he states that obtaining building permits today takes twice as long as fifteen years ago, despite digitalization and a larger number of employees. He particularly referred to the government's 'social' measures, such as price control of fuel and certain food products. He believes that these measures are inefficient and counterproductive, because they subsidize tourists and truck traffic, instead of specifically helping the most vulnerable. He proposes the **Austrian voucher model** for fuel and gas, which would enable targeted assistance to low-income families, instead of indiscriminate price reductions that distort the market. Vanđelić emphasizes that the Government, by regulating the prices of individual products, fails to control the market, but rather creates shortages or changes in product packaging, which is, as he says, 'insane' and 'Stalin did not succeed'.
entities_en: Government of the Republic of Croatia

sections.subtitle: Prevelika i neefikasna država kao uzrok inflacije: Put prema manjoj i digitaliziranoj upravi
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vanđelić objašnjava kako prevelika i neefikasna država doprinosi inflaciji.
sections.content:
Damir Vanđelić ističe da je **prevelika i neefikasna država** glavni uzrok inflacije u Hrvatskoj. Smatra da je država narasla na pedeset posto ekonomije, a pritom je nesposobna. Predlaže tri ključne antiinflacijske mjere:

1.  **Potpuna digitalizacija:** Uvođenje modernih javnonabavnih sustava, poput dinamičkog sustava nabave, kako bi se smanjile mogućnosti korupcije.
2.  **Manja država:** Smanjenje broja zaposlenih u javnom sektoru, koji se u nekim segmentima utrostručio u posljednjih petnaest godina. Država bi se trebala riješiti svega što nije njezina osnovna zadaća (sigurnost, zdravstvo, obrazovanje).
3.  **Profesionalizacija upravljanja:** Strateška državna poduzeća, poput HEP-a, trebaju imati profesionalne uprave s jasnim ciljevima i nadzornim odborima koji prate njihovo ostvarenje. Preostalih 1300 trgovačkih društava u državnom vlasništvu treba prodati.

Vanđelić kritizira i način na koji se država zadužuje, posebno na unutarnjem tržištu, pozivajući građane i mirovinske fondove da kupuju obveznice s niskim kamatama, dok je inflacija znatno viša. Smatra da je to 'muljanje' i da se novac troši bez veze. Zaključuje da bi smanjenje državne potrošnje, pojednostavljenje i ubrzanje procesa te borba protiv korupcije privukli strane investitore i omogućili veće plaće bez inflacije.
sections.subtitle_en: Too large and inefficient state as the cause of inflation: Path towards smaller and digitized administration
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vanđelić explains how an oversized and inefficient state contributes to inflation.
sections.content_en:
Damir Vanđelić points out that an **overly large and inefficient state** is the main cause of inflation in Croatia. He believes that the state has grown to fifty percent of the economy, and is incompetent in the process. He proposes three key anti-inflationary measures:

1.  **Complete digitalization:** Introduction of modern public procurement systems, such as a dynamic procurement system, to reduce opportunities for corruption.
2.  **Smaller state:** Reduction in the number of employees in the public sector, which in some segments has tripled in the last fifteen years. The state should get rid of everything that is not its basic task (security, healthcare, education).
3.  **Professionalization of management:** Strategic state-owned enterprises, such as HEP, should have professional managements with clear goals and supervisory boards that monitor their achievement. The remaining 1300 state-owned commercial companies should be sold.

Vanđelić also criticizes the way the state borrows, especially on the domestic market, calling on citizens and pension funds to buy bonds with low interest rates, while inflation is significantly higher. He believes that this is 'scamming' and that money is being spent unnecessarily. He concludes that a reduction in state spending, simplification and acceleration of processes, and the fight against corruption would attract foreign investors and enable higher wages without inflation.

sections.subtitle: Nedostatak radne snage i strani radnici: Posljedice loših politika
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vanđelić objašnjava da je dolazak stranih radnika posljedica odlaska Hrvata i zapošljavanja u javnom sektoru.
sections.content:
Na pitanje o posljedicama smanjenja državnog aparata, Damir Vanđelić objašnjava da Hrvatska ima **ogroman nedostatak radne snage**, što je rezultat dva glavna faktora: **odlaska hrvatskih građana iz zemlje** i **zapošljavanja u javnom sektoru**. Ističe da kada država podiže plaće u javnom sektoru, to prisiljava i privatnike da čine isto, što umjetno diže cijenu rada i dodatno iscrpljuje tržište rada.

Kao direktnu posljedicu ovih politika, Vanđelić navodi prisutnost između 140.000 i 200.000 stranih radnika u Hrvatskoj. Smatra da su strani radnici dobrodošli i neophodni, posebno u turizmu i prerađivačkoj industriji, ali naglašava da je njihova potreba stvorena. Da Hrvatska nije 'otjerala' pola milijuna svojih građana i da nije bilo masovnog zapošljavanja u javnom sektoru, potreba za stranim radnicima bila bi znatno manja. Vanđelić također jasno razdvaja legalne strane radnike s radnim dozvolama od ilegalnih migranata, ističući da je protiv ilegalnih migracija.
sections.subtitle_en: Labor shortage and foreign workers: Consequences of bad policies
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vanđelić explains that the arrival of foreign workers is a consequence of the departure of Croats and employment in the public sector.
sections.content_en: When asked about the consequences of reducing the state apparatus, Damir Vanđelić explains that Croatia has **a huge shortage of labor**, which is the result of two main factors: **the departure of Croatian citizens from the country** and **employment in the public sector**. He points out that when the state raises salaries in the public sector, it forces private employers to do the same, which artificially raises the price of labor and further exhausts the labor market. As a direct consequence of these policies, Vanđelić cites the presence of between 140,000 and 200,000 foreign workers in Croatia. He believes that foreign workers are welcome and necessary, especially in tourism and the manufacturing industry, but emphasizes that their need was created. If Croatia had not 'driven away' half a million of its citizens and if there had not been massive employment in the public sector, the need for foreign workers would be significantly smaller. Vanđelić also clearly distinguishes legal foreign workers with work permits from illegal migrants, emphasizing that he is against illegal migrations.

sections.subtitle: Erozija dijaloga i kultura straha: Kako se guši kritička misao
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vanđelić govori o kontroli medija i gušenju dijaloga u Hrvatskoj.
sections.content:
Damir Vanđelić izražava zabrinutost zbog **erozije dijaloga** u Hrvatskoj, tvrdeći da je vladajuća stranka, HDZ, preuzela kontrolu nad većinom medija, bilo izravnim dotacijama ili kupovinom. To, prema njemu, stvara okruženje u kojem se kritička misao i pametni prijedlozi guše, a zviždači sustavno uništavaju. Predlaže uvođenje američkog modela za zviždače, gdje bi dobivali značajan postotak spašenog javnog novca, uz javnu zahvalnost i zaštitu.

Vanđelić objašnjava da je **kultura straha** planirana i da se koristi za održavanje kontrole. Navodi primjere premijerovog agresivnog ponašanja prema novinarima, poput pokojnog Mislava Bage, te sustavnog maltretiranja onih koji se usude progovoriti. Smatra da je to 'tjeranje straha' dovelo do toga da ljudi prestaju govoriti, jer vide da su 'pipci svagdje' i da je učinak njihovih riječi minimalan. Zaključuje da je cijeli sustav postao 'pametan koliko je jedan pametan', što je neodrživo za napredak zemlje. Vanđelić ističe da se on osobno ne boji prijetnji, jer se borio za drugačiju Hrvatsku i ne želi da mu premijer prijeti kada upozorava na korupciju.
sections.subtitle_en: Erosion of dialogue and culture of fear: How critical thought is stifled
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vanđelić speaks about media control and the stifling of dialogue in Croatia.
sections.content_en: Damir Vanđelić expresses concern about the **erosion of dialogue** in Croatia, claiming that the ruling party, HDZ, has taken control over most media, either through direct subsidies or purchase. This, according to him, creates an environment in which critical thought and smart proposals are stifled, and whistleblowers are systematically destroyed. He proposes the introduction of the American model for whistleblowers, where they would receive a significant percentage of saved public money, along with public gratitude and protection. Vanđelić explains that the **culture of fear** is planned and used to maintain control. He cites examples of the prime minister's aggressive behavior towards journalists, such as the late Mislav Bago, and the systematic harassment of those who dare to speak out. He believes that this 'instilling of fear' has led people to stop speaking, because they see that 'tentacles are everywhere' and that the effect of their words is minimal. He concludes that the entire system has become 'as smart as one is smart', which is unsustainable for the country's progress. Vanđelić emphasizes that he personally is not afraid of threats, because he fought for a different Croatia and does not want the prime minister to threaten him when he warns about corruption.

iterations.theme_en: Economic difficulties, inefficiency of the state and erosion of dialogue

iterations.theme: Osobna uvjerenja, geopolitika i nada za budućnost Hrvatske

sections.subtitle: „Svi su isti“ – Opasan narativ i geopolitičke zablude
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vangelić objašnjava psihološku pozadinu narativa 'svi su isti'.
sections.content:
Voditeljica postavlja pitanje o narativu „svi su isti“, na što Damir Vangelić objašnjava da je riječ o psihološkoj manipulaciji, vjerojatno osmišljenoj od strane dobrih savjetnika vladajuće stranke HDZ-a. Odbacuje tu tezu uspoređujući svoje sudjelovanje u Domovinskom ratu, unatoč problemima s vidom, s Andrejem Plenkovićem koji nije sudjelovao. Vangelić ističe da kritizira sve stranke kada griješe, a ne samo jednu, te da je Hrvatska trenutno „talac jednog čovjeka“ i njegovog ponašanja.

Kritizira i vanjsku politiku, navodeći suradnju s netradicionalnim saveznicima poput Francuske (kupnja aviona), što dovodi do sličnih poteza Srbije i stvaranja stalne eskalacije. Smatra da su to „idiotski potezi“ i predlaže da se Hrvatska, kao mala zemlja, ugleda na Dubrovačku Republiku – da trguje sa svima, ali da se ne miješa u vojne sukobe. Naglašava da je Hrvatska premala za takve geopolitičke igre.

Unutar vladajuće stranke, Vangelić primjećuje nedostatak inkluzivnog dijaloga, gdje premijer monopolizira raspravu. Ponovno naglašava da „nisu svi isti“, uspoređujući manju korupciju u strankama poput Možemo! (primjer Koste Kostanjevića s 400.000 eura) s milijardama koje se, prema njegovim riječima, kradu u Hrvatskoj, što smatra neusporedivim.
sections.subtitle_en: "Everyone is the same" – Dangerous narrative and geopolitical delusions
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vangelić explains the psychological background of the narrative 'everyone is the same'.
sections.content_en:
The host asks a question about the narrative 'everyone is the same', to which Damir Vangelić explains that it is a psychological manipulation, probably designed by good advisors of the ruling party HDZ. He rejects that thesis by comparing his participation in the Homeland War, despite vision problems, with Andrej Plenković who did not participate. Vangelić points out that he criticizes all parties when they make mistakes, and not just one, and that Croatia is currently 'a hostage of one man' and his behavior.

He also criticizes foreign policy, citing cooperation with non-traditional allies like France (purchase of airplanes), which leads to similar moves by Serbia and the creation of constant escalation. He considers these 'idiotic moves' and suggests that Croatia, as a small country, should emulate the Republic of Dubrovnik – to trade with everyone, but not to interfere in military conflicts. He emphasizes that Croatia is too small for such geopolitical games.

Within the ruling party, Vangelić observes a lack of inclusive dialogue, where the prime minister monopolizes the discussion. He again emphasizes that 'not everyone is the same', comparing smaller corruption in parties like Možemo! (example of Kosta Kostanjević with 400,000 euros) with billions that, according to him, are stolen in Croatia, which he considers incomparable.

sections.subtitle: Geopolitički labirint: Hrvatska između „idiotskih poteza“ i dubrovačke mudrosti
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vangelić objašnjava kako bi Hrvatska trebala slijediti model Dubrovačke Republike u vanjskoj politici.
sections.content:
Damir Vangelić oštro kritizira smjer hrvatske vanjske politike, ističući suradnju s netradicionalnim saveznicima poput Francuske, što se očituje u kupnji aviona. Smatra da takvi potezi izazivaju slične reakcije kod susjeda, poput Srbije koja također nabavlja avione, stvarajući tako **stalnu eskalaciju** tenzija u regiji. Vangelić te poteze naziva „idiotskima“ i predlaže da se Hrvatska, kao mala zemlja, ugleda na povijesni model Dubrovačke Republike.

Prema njegovom mišljenju, Hrvatska bi trebala trgovati sa svima, ali se suzdržavati od vojnih saveza i sukoba, jer je premala da bi se upuštala u takve igre. Upozorava da hrvatski ratni avioni ne bi izdržali ni nekoliko minuta protiv ozbiljne vojne sile. Zalaže se za pametan i neprovokativan pristup u trenutnom geopolitičkom ludilu.

Kritizira i nedostatak unutarnjeg dijaloga unutar vladajuće stranke, gdje premijer, kako kaže, monopolizira raspravu i ne dopušta drugim mišljenjima da dođu do izražaja. Ponovno naglašava da „nisu svi isti“, ističući razlike u razini korupcije između političkih opcija, gdje se manji slučajevi korupcije ne mogu uspoređivati sa **sistemskom korupcijom** koja se mjeri u milijardama.
sections.subtitle_en: Geopolitical labyrinth: Croatia between „idiotic moves“ and Dubrovnik wisdom
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vangelić explains how Croatia should follow the model of the Dubrovnik Republic in foreign policy.
sections.content_en: Damir Vangelić sharply criticizes the direction of Croatian foreign policy, highlighting cooperation with non-traditional allies like France, which is evident in the purchase of airplanes. He believes that such moves provoke similar reactions from neighbors, such as Serbia which is also acquiring airplanes, thus creating a **constant escalation** of tensions in the region. Vangelić calls these moves 'idiotic' and suggests that Croatia, as a small country, should emulate the historical model of the Republic of Dubrovnik. In his opinion, Croatia should trade with everyone, but refrain from military alliances and conflicts, because it is too small to engage in such games. He warns that Croatian warplanes would not last even a few minutes against a serious military power. He advocates for a smart and non-provocative approach in the current geopolitical madness. He also criticizes the lack of internal dialogue within the ruling party, where the prime minister, as he says, monopolizes the discussion and does not allow other opinions to be expressed. He again emphasizes that 'not everyone is the same,' highlighting differences in the level of corruption between political options, where smaller cases of corruption cannot be compared to **systemic corruption** measured in billions.

sections.subtitle: Prijetnje, pritisci i ratna trauma kao pokretač borbe za pravednu Hrvatsku
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vangelić emotivno govori o svojim ratnim iskustvima i motivaciji za politički angažman.
sections.content:
Damir Vangelić otvoreno govori o **prijetnjama i pritiscima** s kojima se suočava zbog svog kritičkog stava. Prisjeća se kako mu je jedan ministar prenio prijetnje od premijera, sugerirajući da će izgubiti poslovne prilike ako se politički poveže s oporbenim strankama poput MOST-a ili Miroslava Škore. Međutim, te su ga prijetnje samo dodatno motivirale da se aktivnije uključi u politiku i bori protiv takvih praksi.

Opisuje i razne oblike maltretiranja, poput carinskih inspekcija u njegovoj uljari i inspekcija zaštite na radu na njegovom OPG-u, koje doživljava kao orkestrirane pokušaje zastrašivanja i diskreditacije. Smatra da je cilj takvih akcija potvrditi narativ „svi su isti“.

Njegova motivacija duboko je ukorijenjena u ratnom iskustvu – svjedočenju smrti dvanaestorice ljudi u jednom danu i šestomjesečnom zarobljeništvu. Vangelić ističe da se ti ljudi nisu borili za „ovakvu državu“ te odbija biti zastrašen od strane premijera koji nije sudjelovao u ratu. Izražava duboku frustraciju zbog nepravde i korupcije, posebno spominjući **„vaučerske privatizacije“** iz devedesetih, kada su se neki obogatili dok su se drugi borili. Boji se da bi se takav scenarij ponovio u slučaju novog sukoba.

Njegova obitelj pruža mu punu podršku, a on sam želi biti primjer svojoj djeci, pokazujući im da se treba boriti za bolje društvo, čak i protiv „intelektualnih papaka“ i „kukavica“ na vlasti.
sections.subtitle_en: Threats, pressures and war trauma as the driver of the struggle for a just Croatia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vangelić emotionally speaks about his war experiences and motivation for political engagement.
sections.content_en: Damir Vangelić openly speaks about **threats and pressures** he faces due to his critical stance. He recalls how a minister conveyed threats from the prime minister to him, suggesting that he would lose business opportunities if he politically associated with opposition parties like MOST or Miroslav Škore. However, these threats only further motivated him to become more actively involved in politics and fight against such practices. He also describes various forms of harassment, such as customs inspections in his oil mill and occupational safety inspections at his OPG, which he perceives as orchestrated attempts at intimidation and discredit. He believes that the goal of such actions is to confirm the narrative 'everyone is the same'. His motivation is deeply rooted in his war experience – witnessing the death of twelve people in one day and six months of captivity. Vangelić emphasizes that these people did not fight for 'a state like this' and refuses to be intimidated by a prime minister who did not participate in the war. He expresses deep frustration due to injustice and corruption, especially mentioning **'voucher privatizations'** from the nineties, when some got rich while others fought. He fears that such a scenario would repeat itself in the event of a new conflict. His family provides him full support, and he himself wants to be an example to his children, showing them that one must fight for a better society, even against 'intellectual wimps' and 'cowards' in power.

sections.subtitle: Mandati dijaspore: Izborni inženjering ili autentičan glas Hrvata izvan domovine?
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vangelić objašnjava svoj stav o pravu glasa dijaspore u Saboru.
sections.content:
Voditeljica postavlja pitanje o nedavnoj raspravi o povećanju broja mandata dijaspore u Hrvatskom saboru. Damir Vangelić to odmah prepoznaje kao **„izborni inženjering“** HDZ-a, taktiku manipulacije izbornim zakonima. Iako se slaže da Hrvati izvan domovine trebaju imati svoj glas u Saboru, možda nekoliko predstavnika iz različitih zemalja (Australije, Kanade, Bosne i Hercegovine), naglašava ključnu razliku.

Prema Vangeliću, ti predstavnici bi trebali **glasovati isključivo o pitanjima koja se tiču Hrvata u inozemstvu**, a ne o unutarnjim hrvatskim pitanjima poput državnog proračuna, PDV-a ili same vlade. Objašnjava da građani koji žive u Hrvatskoj plaćaju poreze i imaju vojnu obvezu, čime stječu pravo odlučivanja o domaćoj politici. Članovi dijaspore koji ne plaćaju porez u Hrvatskoj ne bi trebali imati to isto pravo. Njihova uloga trebala bi biti savjetodavna, prenoseći iskustva i probleme, posebno kada je riječ o Hrvatima u Vojvodini ili Bosni i Hercegovini.

Kritizira i trenutnu politiku prema Hrvatima u Bosni i Hercegovini, gdje se Hrvatska, umjesto s tradicionalnim saveznicima poput SAD-a i Njemačke, svrstava uz Republiku Srpsku. Vangelić sumnja da je povećanje mandata dijaspore potez koji će koristiti HDZ-u, budući da mnogi iseljeni Hrvati kritički gledaju na aktualnu vlast. Predviđa daljnje manipulacije kako bi ti mandati služili interesima vladajuće stranke. Zaključuje da bi se, ako članovi dijaspore žele utjecati na svakodnevni život u Hrvatskoj, trebali vratiti u domovinu.
sections.subtitle_en: Diaspora mandates: Electoral engineering or authentic voice of Croats outside the homeland?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vangelić explains his stance on the right to vote of the diaspora in the Parliament.
sections.content_en: The host asks a question about the recent discussion regarding increasing the number of diaspora mandates in the Croatian Parliament. Damir Vangelić immediately recognizes this as the HDZ's **'electoral engineering'**, a tactic of manipulating electoral laws. Although he agrees that Croats outside the homeland should have their voice in Parliament, perhaps a few representatives from different countries (Australia, Canada, Bosnia and Herzegovina), he emphasizes a key difference. According to Vangelić, these representatives should **vote exclusively on issues concerning Croats abroad**, and not on internal Croatian issues such as the state budget, VAT, or the government itself. He explains that citizens living in Croatia pay taxes and have military obligation, thereby acquiring the right to decide on domestic policy. Diaspora members who do not pay taxes in Croatia should not have that same right. Their role should be advisory, conveying experiences and problems, especially when it comes to Croats in Vojvodina or Bosnia and Herzegovina. He also criticizes the current policy towards Croats in Bosnia and Herzegovina, where Croatia, instead of with traditional allies like the USA and Germany, aligns itself with Republika Srpska. Vangelić doubts that the increase in diaspora mandates is a move that will benefit the HDZ, since many emigrated Croats view the current government critically. He predicts further manipulations so that these mandates serve the interests of the ruling party. He concludes that if diaspora members want to influence everyday life in Croatia, they should return to the homeland.

sections.subtitle: Ratna iskustva kao moralni kompas i osuda povijesnog revizionizma
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vangelić objašnjava simboliku hrvatske zastave i svoj duboki osjećaj domoljublja.
sections.content:
Na pitanje voditeljice o utjecaju njegovog ratnog iskustva (dvanaest poginulih, šest mjeseci zarobljeništva), Damir Vangelić odgovara da ga je to iskustvo **„formiralo i učvrstilo“**, čineći ga duboko svjesnim nepravde i zla. Vjeruje da nečinjenje omogućuje zlu da prevlada.

Svoj duboki patriotizam, koji se očituje u stalnom isticanju hrvatske zastave na njegovom imanju, suprotstavlja navodnom nedostatku istinske povezanosti vladajuće elite s domovinom. Naglašava da hrvatska zastava predstavlja sve građane, uključujući i pripadnike drugih nacionalnosti (Srbe, Albance) koji su se borili uz njega.

Potom se osvrće na **povijesni revizionizam**, ističući da nije bio ni u partizanima ni u ustašama. Osuđuje sve zločine, posebno ubijanje ratnih zarobljenika i civila, navodeći primjere masovnih grobnica i logora Jasenovac. Kritizira poslijeratni komunistički režim zbog izostanka pravih suđenja (poput Nürnberških procesa) i počinjenja novih zločina, što je, prema njegovom mišljenju, dodatno potaknulo ustaštvo.

Objašnjava kontekstualnu prirodu izbora tijekom Drugog svjetskog rata: odlazak u ustaše ako su u blizini bili četnički zločini, ili u antifašističke pokrete u Istri protiv talijanske okupacije. Odlučno tvrdi da je ubijanje čak i jednog djeteta neprihvatljivo, odbacujući svako opravdavanje zločina brojkama. Smatra da je stalno vraćanje na povijesne podjele **politička taktika za stvaranje „magle“** i odvraćanje pažnje. Izražava sreću zbog hrvatske neovisnosti, uspoređujući je s nejednakostima u Titovoj Jugoslaviji, gdje su komunističke elite živjele dobro dok su obični ljudi patili.
sections.subtitle_en: War experiences as a moral compass and condemnation of historical revisionism
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vangelić explains the symbolism of the Croatian flag and his deep feeling of patriotism.
sections.content_en:
To the host's question about the impact of his war experience (twelve killed, six months of captivity), Damir Vangelić answers that this experience **"formed and strengthened"** him, making him deeply aware of injustice and evil. He believes that inaction allows evil to prevail.

He contrasts his deep patriotism, which is manifested in the constant display of the Croatian flag on his property, with the alleged lack of true connection of the ruling elite with the homeland. He emphasizes that the Croatian flag represents all citizens, including members of other nationalities (Serbs, Albanians) who fought alongside him.

Then he refers to **historical revisionism**, pointing out that he was neither with the Partisans nor with the Ustaše. He condemns all crimes, especially the killing of prisoners of war and civilians, citing examples of mass graves and the Jasenovac camp. He criticizes the post-war communist regime for the absence of proper trials (like the Nuremberg trials) and the commission of new crimes, which, in his opinion, further encouraged Ustaše sentiment.

He explains the contextual nature of choices during World War II: joining the Ustaše if Chetnik crimes were nearby, or joining anti-fascist movements in Istria against Italian occupation. He firmly states that killing even one child is unacceptable, rejecting any justification of crimes by numbers. He believes that constantly returning to historical divisions is a **political tactic to create "fog"** and distract attention. He expresses happiness for Croatian independence, comparing it with inequalities in Tito's Yugoslavia, where communist elites lived well while ordinary people suffered.

sections.subtitle: Politička lustracija i poziv na promjenu: Nada za budućnost Hrvatske
sections.screenshot_description: Damir Vangelić upućuje snažan apel za odlazak starih elita i dolazak novih, sposobnih ljudi.
sections.content:
Na pitanje voditeljice postoji li nada za Hrvatsku, Damir Vangelić odlučno potvrđuje da nade ima, ali uz uvjet **„političke lustracije“**. Ne misli pritom na zakonsku lustraciju, već na društveno shvaćanje da stare, nesposobne elite moraju odstupiti s vlasti. Uspoređuje hrvatske elite sa slovenskim, navodeći da su čak i ako su obje ukorijenjene u komunističkim strukturama, slovenski „Udbaši“ „bolji“ jer su uspješniji.

Poziva na davanje prilike kompetentnim, mladim ljudima da oblikuju budućnost. Strastveno ističe da Hrvatska, nacija s 1300 godina povijesti, konačno ima svoju državu, a sadašnje je elite uništavaju tjerajući ljude iz nje. Izravno se obraća aktualnom vodstvu riječima: **„Maknite se. Please. Ne bud dobro.“** (Maknite se. Molim vas. Neće biti dobro.) Zaključuje snažnim pozivom na promjenu i bolju budućnost za Hrvatsku.
sections.subtitle_en: Political lustration and a call for change: Hope for the future of Croatia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Damir Vangelić issues a strong appeal for the departure of old elites and the arrival of new, capable people.
sections.content_en: To the host's question if there is hope for Croatia, Damir Vangelić decisively confirms that there is hope, but with the condition of **"political lustration"**. He does not mean legal lustration by this, but a social understanding that old, incompetent elites must step down from power. He compares Croatian elites with Slovenian ones, stating that even if both are rooted in communist structures, the Slovenian "Udbaši" are "better" because they are more successful. He calls for giving an opportunity to competent, young people to shape the future. He passionately emphasizes that Croatia, a nation with 1300 years of history, finally has its own state, and the current elites are destroying it by driving people out of it. He directly addresses the current leadership with the words: **"Maknite se. Please. Ne bud dobro."** (Get out. Please. It will not be good.) He concludes with a strong call for change and a better future for Croatia.

iterations.theme_en: Personal beliefs, geopolitics and hope for the future of Croatia
